arts.ttl: Brendan Rodgers urges Celtic to have no regrets in Bayern return as boss already straining at the Munich leash
arts.sum: The Irishman admitted his side lacked aggression for much of the game before coming alive in the latter stages

arts.ttl: Olise, Kane fire Bayern to first-leg win over Celtic
arts.sum: Bayern Munich scored either side of halftime through Michael Olise and Harry Kane but had to endure late pressure from hosts Celtic before securing a 2-1 victory in their Champions League playoff first leg on Wednesday.

arts.ttl: Champions League round-up: Bayern edge Celtic as Harry Kane shines
arts.sum: The first-legs of the Champions League play-off ties are complete after eight clubs faced off on Wednesday night. It wasn’t as thrilling as Tuesday night’s action when Jude...

arts.ttl: 'Game on again' - Maeda pulls one back for Celtic against Bayern
arts.sum: Celtic's Daizen Maeda halves the deficit in their play-off first-leg tie against Bayern Munich to keep their hopes of reaching the Champions League last 16 for the first time since 2013.

arts.ttl: Harry Kane: "When you win a game like this you can be pleased"
arts.sum: Harry Kane shared his thoughts in the aftermath of Bayern Munich's narrow first-leg win over reigning Scottish champions Celtic.

arts.ttl: Celtic know the exact UEFA charge they are staring down over 'show Israel the red card' banner
arts.sum: The governing body have clamped the Parkhead side in the past over their support of Palestine and appear set to do so again

arts.ttl: Celtic team news vs Bayern Munich earns fan reaction as Brendan Rodgers makes Daizen Maeda call
arts.sum: Celtic starting XI: SCHMEICHEL, JOHNSTON, CCV, TRUSTY, TAYLOR, MCGREGOR, ENGELS, HATATE, MAEDA, KUHN, IDAH. What fans said about Celtic’s starting lineup vs Bayern Munich

arts.ttl: Chris Sutton reacts to Celtic's defeat to Bayern Munich and what happened after Harry Kane's goal
arts.sum: Chris Sutton says Celtic improved after Harry Kane’s goal for Bayern Munich ‘After Bayern Munich got the second, Celtic played with the desired bravery and kept fighting.

arts.ttl: Maeda gives Celtic Champions League hope but Kane wins first leg for Bayern
arts.sum: At 2-0 down, Celtic were staring at the invidious scenario of a box-ticking exercise in Munich next week. Their response, clawing their way back into this tie via Daizen Maeda, permitted fresh hope. Bayern Munich will remain the favourites to progress to the last 16. Indeed, for 78 minutes they looked irritated at being forced to play an extra couple of games to get there. Yet Celtic are due praise for their refusal to wilt against such stellar opposition. They will believe they can cause further Bayern scares in the Allianz Arena. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich edge past Celtic in UEFA Champions League knockouts play-off first leg
arts.sum: Harry Kane was on target as Bayern Munich recorded a vital 2-1 win in the first leg of their UEFA Champions League last 16 play-off away to Celtic. Bayern started the second half confidently, with a flurry of corners in the opening minutes resulting in them scoring their second of the game through Kane, who volleyed home from inside the six-yard box at the far post.
